_comment_1: PROJECT TIER — copy to <project>/.claude/settings.json in every Builder repo (the org-template repo should ship this by default, per Builder Flow Step 3: Provisioning). Checked into version control, reviewed in PRs, editable by the Builder but visible to everyone. This tier implements the 'Ask' and 'Monitor' rows of company-agent-guardrails' Recommended Default Stance — the 'Deny' row belongs at the managed tier (see managed-settings.example.json) so it can't be locally removed.
_comment_2: Verify exact key/glob syntax against https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions before relying on this in production.
_comment_3: Monitor tier (all shell execution, file writes, external API calls) is not a blocking mechanism — it's satisfied by the audit-logging skill + Claude Code's own session transcript. No settings.json entry needed; see docs/incident-response.md and keshet-builder-skills/audit-logging/SKILL.md for what must be logged and where.
